mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with increasing residential housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly GDP growth signals strengthening corporate revenue trends, which translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month when inflation remains contained.
failure_condition: The combined growth-inflation signal predicts negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in real personal savings exceeding 10 billion dollars indicates robust consumer balance sheet health, which historically drives positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns do not exceed baseline by at least 0.5% when RSAFS surges monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production confirms broad-based economic momentum, reducing recession risk and lifting equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint payroll-production signal yields a negative directional accuracy below 50% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain normalization phases, rising industrial output without corresponding price inflation expands corporate margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Rising INDPRO combined with stable CPIAUCSL fails to generate positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month increase in employment levels signals structural labor market recovery, which steadily supports corporate earnings and equity valuations over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy for PAYEMS growth remains below 52% over any 18-month rolling window.
